Title: Rubber biosynthesis drives the biogenesis and development of rubber particles, the rubber-producing organelles

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rubber particles in latex/laticifer cells; roots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage polymer and defense-associated latex component in rubber-producing plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of TkCPTL1 completely abolishes natural rubber biosynthesis and rubber particle biogenesis/ontogeny.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPTL1 is a latex-predominant CPT-like gene in TK20. 2. TkCPTL1 interacts with TkCPT1, TkCPT2, and TkREF. 3. CRISPR knockout of TkCPTL1 eliminates rubber filaments, latex rubber phase, oil red O/I-Br staining signals, and strongly reduces latex solids, indicating loss of NR and rubber particles.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; expression analysis; split-ubiquitin membrane yeast two-hybrid; split-luciferase complementation; latex phenotyping; oil red O staining; I-Br staining; high-speed centrifugation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth conditions; latex and root analyses in 3-month-old plants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious aboveground or belowground growth phenotype reported in knockout lines under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target for engineering rubber production and rubber particle formation, but knockout abolishes product entirely; suitable as an essential complex component to preserve or enhance in rubber breeding/engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as rubber transferase activator/anchoring component rather than a catalytic CPT enzyme; authors conclude it anchors TkCPTs to rubber particles via interaction with TkREF and TkCPTs.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rubber particles in latex/laticifer cells; roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: rubber storage polymer and defense-associated latex metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TkCPT1 promotes rubber biosynthesis; single knockout significantly reduces root rubber content and large rubber particles, while contribution is partially redundant with TkCPT2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT1 is a latex-specific cis-prenyltransferase highly expressed in latex. 2. TkCPT1 localizes to the ER when transiently expressed, with additional nuclear signal in tobacco leaves. 3. CRISPR knockout of TkCPT1 significantly reduces rubber content and large rubber particle abundance without affecting obvious plant growth. 4. Combined loss with TkCPT2 completely blocks NR biosynthesis and abolishes rubber particle formation, demonstrating functional redundancy and essential pathway role.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue expression analysis; subcellular localization in N. benthamiana; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; root rubber quantification; I-Br staining; oil red O staining; Nile red latex imaging; latex centrifugation; cryo-SEM; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: rubber chain elongation on rubber particle surface
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-1,4-polyisoprene chain elongation during natural rubber biosynthesis using IPP units and an allylic diphosphate initiator within the rubber transferase complex.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: isopentenyl pyrophosphate (IPP) plus allylic diphosphate initiator (likely FPP or GGPP)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as essential core catalytic step for NR production; decisive control point together with TkCPT2, though individual rate limitation not explicitly established.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically and highly expressed in latex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically and highly expressed in latex
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; also nucleus in transient tobacco assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth conditions; fresh latex and roots from 3-month-old plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible growth/development defects in single knockout under tested conditions; reduced rubber may affect ecological fitness/herbivory based on prior literature.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance natural rubber biosynthesis, but likely requires stacking/coordination with TkCPT2 and TkCPTL1 because of redundancy and complex-dependent function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TkCPT1 and TkCPT2 share 96.10% sequence identity and highly similar predicted structures, supporting functional redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rubber particles in latex/laticifer cells; roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: rubber storage polymer and defense-associated latex metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TkCPT2 acts redundantly with TkCPT1 in natural rubber biosynthesis; together with TkCPT1 it is essential for NR production and rubber particle development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT2 is a latex-specific cis-prenyltransferase highly expressed in latex. 2. TkCPT2 localizes to the ER, with additional nuclear signal in transient tobacco assays. 3. Loss of both TkCPT1 and TkCPT2 completely eliminates rubber biosynthesis, abolishes detectable rubber particles, and remodels latex proteome/metabolome. 4. TkCPT2 is therefore an essential redundant catalytic component of the rubber transferase machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue expression analysis; subcellular localization in N. benthamiana; CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout with TkCPT1; root rubber quantification; I-Br staining; oil red O staining; Nile red latex imaging; latex centrifugation; cryo-SEM; TEM; proteomics; metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: rubber chain elongation on rubber particle surface
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-1,4-polyisoprene chain elongation during natural rubber biosynthesis using IPP units and an allylic diphosphate initiator within the rubber transferase complex.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: isopentenyl pyrophosphate (IPP) plus allylic diphosphate initiator (likely FPP or GGPP)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as essential core catalytic step together with TkCPT1; individual key-control status not separately resolved.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically and highly expressed in latex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically and highly expressed in latex
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; also nucleus in transient tobacco assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth conditions; fresh latex and roots from 3-month-old plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible growth/development defect reported for double knockout, but complete loss of rubber may affect ecological fitness/herbivory.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target only in combination with TkCPT1/TkCPTL1 and other rubber transferase complex components due to redundancy and complex dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Nearly identical orthologues are conserved across Taraxacum species; predicted structure highly similar to TkCPT1.
